core_philosophy: Expectancy over Accuracy: The percentage of winning trades is largely irrelevant. A system that is right only 30% of the time can be highly profitable if the winning trades (rewards) are significantly larger than the losing trades (risks).
The Holy Grail is Position Sizing: Success comes from psychological discipline, strict risk management, and mathematically defined position sizing, not from a magic entry or 'stock picking' signal.

rule_1_initialization_of_base_risk_1R.definition: The system must predefine the initial risk (1R) for every trade, which represents the maximum worst-case capital loss allowed per unit traded, excluding slippage and gap openings.
rule_1_initialization_of_base_risk_1R.execution_constraint: The algorithm MUST establish an initial stop-loss price prior to entry. 1R serves as the absolute baseline benchmark against which all subsequent potential gains and losses are measured.

rule_2_computation_of_trade_outcomes.definition: All projected and realized trade outcomes must be calculated strictly as multiples of the initial risk (R-Multiples), completely disregarding raw percentage price gains or absolute asset values.
rule_2_computation_of_trade_outcomes.mathematical_formula: R-Multiple = (Exit_Price - Entry_Price) / 1R (adjust sign for short positions to ensure profit is positive)
rule_2_computation_of_trade_outcomes.execution_constraint: Winning trades yield positive R-multiples (e.g., generating $1,000 on a trade with a $500 1R yields a 2-R multiple), and losing trades yield negative R-multiples.

rule_3_calculation_of_system_expectancy.definition: The agent must continuously calculate Expectancy to mathematically determine the average amount of money the system will generate per dollar risked over a series of trades.
rule_3_calculation_of_system_expectancy.variables: PW = Probability of a winning trade, AW = Average gain as positive R-multiple, PL = Probability of a losing trade, AL = Average loss as positive R-multiple.
rule_3_calculation_of_system_expectancy.execution_constraint: The agent must only deploy capital into systems and setups where Expectancy > 0.

rule_4_evaluation_via_asymmetric_payoffs.definition: The algorithmic agent must evaluate and optimize for trades that yield large, asymmetric R-multiple profits (e.g., 10-R or 20-R), explicitly overriding the percentage win rate (reliability) and percentage price returns.
rule_4_evaluation_via_asymmetric_payoffs.logic: A trade profile with a low win rate (e.g., 36%) but high asymmetric R-multiples MUST be mathematically prioritized over a system with a high win rate (e.g., 90%) but negative or low R-multiples.
rule_4_evaluation_via_asymmetric_payoffs.execution_constraint: The agent must define the tightness of the 1R stop; a tighter 1R stop makes the same target price movement yield an exponentially larger R-multiple. The agent must strictly abort trades at 1R to cut losses short, while using trailing stops to let profits run.

rule_5_normalization_of_position_sizing.definition: The agent must utilize 1R to normalize position sizing across diverse asset classes, ensuring strict risk parity regardless of the underlying asset's price or percentage volatility.
rule_5_normalization_of_position_sizing.execution_constraint: The agent must equate a 1R risk to the exact same percentage of total account equity (e.g., 1%) across all positions. This mathematically isolates the algorithm from the raw percentage price changes of the underlying assets.

step_1_system_equity_and_risk_limit.objective: Calculate the maximum absolute dollar amount of capital that can be lost on a single trade based on current equity.
step_1_system_equity_and_risk_limit.logic: The Percent Risk Model mandates risking only a constant percentage of current total account equity on any given position.

step_2_market_volatility_atr.objective: Quantify the daily price fluctuation (noise) of the underlying asset to ensure protective stops are placed objectively.
step_2_market_volatility_atr.logic: Determine the Average True Range (ATR), which factors in gap openings.

step_3_establish_volatility_stop_1R.objective: Define the worst-case exit point, establishing the initial risk (1R) on a per-share basis.
step_3_establish_volatility_stop_1R.logic: Multiply the calculated ATR by a constant multiplier (e.g., 3.0x ATR) to keep the stop safely outside of normal daily market noise.

step_4_execute_position_sizing.objective: Determine the exact number of shares to purchase by equating the per-share risk to the total allowable portfolio risk.
step_4_execute_position_sizing.logic: Divide the total allowable dollar risk by the per-share dollar risk.

step_5_implement_volatility_trailing_sto.objective: Lock in profits as the market moves in favor of the trade while strictly limiting downside.
step_5_implement_volatility_trailing_sto.logic: Recalculate the volatility stop daily based on the asset's closing price. The trailing stop must only move in the direction of the trade.
